I completely agree that the justice system needs improvement, especially when it comes to ensuring representation for diverse groups. It's crucial to address unconscious bias within juries as well.

That’s a great point. Unconscious biases can significantly impact how cases are decided, especially if the jury isn’t representative of the community it serves. One potential solution could be implementing diverse juries through randomized selection processes that prioritize diversity. This would help ensure that different perspectives are considered during deliberations and increase trust in the legal system among marginalized communities.
